requirements.description: Human intent and machine-verifiable acceptance are stored separately and validated together.
acceptance_steps: Initialize both project files without overwriting existing state.
Reject placeholders, mismatched state, stale resume state, and unsupported passing evidence.
Accept a complete structurally valid active state.

evidence.summary: Eight deterministic tests passed, including dual-file initialization, stale resume rejection, evidence enforcement, blocker recovery, and completion gating.

requirements.description: Failure handling classifies errors before retrying and prevents repeated semantic attempts.
acceptance_steps: The skill distinguishes transient, reasoning-recoverable, user-fixable, and unexpected failures.
A fresh agent refuses an unchanged third attempt after repeated semantic failure.

evidence.summary: Fresh Luna agents reconciled an ambiguous external write and refused an unchanged third semantic retry in favor of authoritative transition tracing.

requirements.description: Delegation occurs only for independent work whose expected contribution exceeds coordination cost.
acceptance_steps: The skill defines a conjunctive delegation admission gate.
Fresh-agent tests keep sequential critical-path work local and allow bounded parallel work.

evidence.summary: A fresh Luna agent kept tightly coupled critical-path diagnosis local, rejected duplicate diagnosis, and delegated only the disjoint read-only acceptance lane.

requirements.description: The public package passes deterministic, behavioral, privacy, and GitHub CI verification.
acceptance_steps: Run the repository test suite and Codex skill validator.
Run privacy scanning and fresh-agent behavioral scenarios.
Push the release and confirm GitHub Actions succeeds.

evidence.summary: Communication-loop release commit 84ffb77 passed repository tests and GitHub Actions validation after local 13-test verification.

requirements.description: The exact published release installs locally without duplicating global instructions.
acceptance_steps: Run the public installer twice against an isolated Codex home.
Install locally and compare all installed skill file hashes with the repository.
Confirm one managed global instruction block remains.

evidence.ref: python scripts/install.py; SHA256 comparison of C:\Users\paras\.codex\skills\outcome-integrity against source; active AGENTS.md managed block inspection
evidence.summary: The updated outcome-integrity skill installed into the active Codex home; installed non-cache skill files match source hashes and the active global rules include the layer-separated status rule.

requirements.description: The skill separates the final outcome and proof from intermediate methods before substantive work, invalidates stale contracts after correction, and replans after a rejected method.
acceptance_steps: Assert the pre-work outcome frame, counterfactual, and no-capability-narrowing rules in the skill and global instructions.
Assert corrections invalidate dependent task contracts and rejected methods trigger replanning.
Run package tests, the Codex skill validator, local installation hash verification, and public CI.

evidence.summary: Ten package tests, the Codex skill validator, public GitHub Actions, exact local installation hashes, and a single managed global rule block passed for commit 143d005.

requirements.description: Recurring or autonomous work is admitted only with bounded side effects, resource and retention limits, progress-aware retries, persistent restart behavior, and a fail-closed no-progress transition.
acceptance_steps: Assert a concise bounded-autonomy gate in the skill and global rules without adding an orchestration runtime.
Assert the project template captures progress identity, resource limits, retention, restart, cancellation, and no-progress recovery.
Run package tests, Codex skill validation, public CI, exact installation hash verification, and the completion gate.

evidence.summary: Eleven package tests, official skill validation, Python compilation, privacy scanning, public CI, exact five-file installation hashes, and one managed global rule block passed for commit c0c46d2.

requirements.description: Simple user questions receive their plain-language conclusion before technical detail, process narration, or an unnecessary plan.
acceptance_steps: Add a concise direct-answer-first rule to the skill, global instructions, and default prompt.
Require ambiguous terms to be distinguished briefly without forcing the user to simplify their question.
Add and pass a regression test for the rule.

evidence.ref: TEMP/TMP=C:\tmp python -m unittest discover -s tests -v; project_outcome.py validate --root .; project_outcome.py resume --root .
evidence.summary: Thirteen deterministic tests passed, preserving direct-answer-first wording across the skill, global rules, documentation, and default prompt; active project state also validated and resumed.

requirements.description: Confusing status replies are treated as drift: the agent separates real outcome, tooling or restart state, model state, and communication state, then stops explanation loops with a short conclusion/distinction/next-action frame.
acceptance_steps: Add a concise confusing-reply-loop rule to the skill and global instructions.
Require status language to name separate project, tooling, restart/model, and communication layers.
Add and pass a regression test for the rule.

evidence.ref: TEMP/TMP=C:\tmp python -m unittest discover -s tests -v; project_outcome.py validate --root .; project_outcome.py resume --root .
evidence.summary: Thirteen deterministic tests passed, including confusing-reply-loop and layer-separated status wording across the skill, global rules, documentation, and default prompt; active project state also validated and resumed.

requirements.description: An active project retains Codex ownership across questions, corrections, and noisy prompts, and resumes the next safe already-authorized action without repeated user management.
acceptance_steps: Require a compact control frame and message classification in the skill and global rules.
Require questions and corrections to update rather than cancel the active project.
Require same-turn continuation of safe authorized work while respecting explicit pause, diagnosis-only, authorization, and blocker boundaries.
Pass package tests, project validation, public CI, installation, and exact installed-source hash verification.
